Items.Title: JavaScript 数组方法解释 | JavaScript Array methods explained
Items.Description:
马尔科·丹尼克

发表于返回一个新数组，该数组是该数组与其他数组和/或值的连接。

 const fruits = [ ' 🍉 ' , ' 🍎 ' , ' 🍒 ' , ' 🍌 ' ]; const vegetables = [ ' 🧅 ' , ' 🌽 ' , ' 🥕 ' , ' 🥑 ' ]; // 1. concat() const food = fruits . concat ( vegetables ); // ["🍉", "🍎", "🍒", "🍌", "🧅", "🌽", "🥕", "🥑"]

复制数组中的一系列数组元素。

 // 2. copyWithin() const fruits = [ ' 🍉 ' , ' 🍎 ' , ' 🍒 ' , ' 🍌 ' ]; const fruitsCopy = fruits . copyWithin ( 0 , 2 ); // ["🍒", "🍌", "🍒", "🍌"]

如果此数组中的每个元素都满足测试函数，则返回true 。

 // 3. every() const fruits = [ ' 🍉 ' , ' 🍎 ' , ' 🍒 ' , ' 🍌 ' ]; const allBananas = fruits . every ( fruit => fruit === ' 🍌 ' ); // false

使用静态值填充数组中从开始索引到结束索引的所有元素。

 // 4. fill() const fruits = [ ' 🍉 ' , ' 🍎 ' , ' 🍒 ' , ' 🍌 ' ]; const lemons = fruits . fill ( ' 🍋 ' ); // ["🍋", "🍋", "🍋", "🍋"]

返回一个新数组，其中包含调用数组中提供的过滤函数为其返回true的所有元素。

 // 5. filter() const fruits = [ ' 🍉 ' , ' 🍎 ' , ' 🍒 ' , ' 🍌 ' ]; const onlyBananas = [ ' 🍉 ' , ' 🍎 ' , ' 🍌 ' , ' 🍌 ' ]. filter ( fruit => fruit === ' 🍌 ' ); // ["🍌", "🍌"]

如果数组中的某个元素满足测试函数，则返回数组中找到的element ，如果没有找到，则返回undefined 。

 // 6. find() const fruits = [ ' 🍉 ' , ' 🍎 ' , ' 🍒 ' , ' 🍌 ' ]; const cherry = fruits . find ( fruit => fruit === ' 🍒 ' ); // "🍒"

如果数组中的元素满足测试函数，则返回数组中找到的索引，如果未找到，则返回-1 。

 // 7. findIndex() const fruits = [ ' 🍉 ' , ' 🍎 ' , ' 🍒 ' , ' 🍌 ' ]; const cherryIndex = fruits . findIndex ( fruit => fruit === ' 🍒 ' ); // 2

为数组中的每个元素调用一个函数。

 // 8. forEach() const vegetables = [ ' 🧅 ' , ' 🌽 ' , ' 🥕 ' , ' 🥑 ' ]; vegetables . forEach ( vegetable => console . log ( vegetable )); // "🥦" // "🌽" // "🥕" // "🥑"

确定数组是否包含值，根据需要返回true或false 。

 // 9. includes() const vegetables = [ ' 🧅 ' , ' 🌽 ' , ' 🥕 ' , ' 🥑 ' ]; const includesCorn = vegetables . includes ( ' 🌽 ' ); // true const includesTomato = vegetables . includes ( ' 🍅 ' ); // false

将数组的所有元素连接成一个字符串。

 // 10. join() const vegetables = [ ' 🧅 ' , ' 🌽 ' , ' 🥕 ' , ' 🥑 ' ]; const vegetablesGroup = vegetables . join ( '' ); // "🥦🌽🥕🥑"

返回一个新数组，其中包含对该数组中每个元素调用函数的结果。

 // 11. map() const vegetables = [ ' 🧅 ' , ' 🌽 ' , ' 🥕 ' , ' 🥑 ' ]; const doubledVegetables = vegetables . map ( vegetable => vegetable + vegetable ); // ["🥦🥦", "🌽🌽", "🥕🥕", "🥑🥑"]

将一个或多个元素添加到数组的末尾，并返回数组的新长度。

 // 12. push() const fruits = [ ' 🍉 ' , ' 🍎 ' , ' 🍒 ' , ' 🍌 ' ]; fruits . push ( ' 🥝 ' ); // ["🍉", "🍎", "🍒", "🍌", "🥝"]

反转数组元素的顺序。

 // 13. reverse() const fruits = [ ' 🍉 ' , ' 🍎 ' , ' 🍒 ' , ' 🍌 ' ]; const fruits = [ ' 🍉 ' , ' 🍎 ' , ' 🍒 ' , ' 🍌 ' ]; const reversedFruits = fruits . reverse (); // ["🍌", "🍒", "🍎", "🍉"]

提取调用数组的一部分并返回一个新数组。

 // 14. slice() const fruits = [ ' 🍉 ' , ' 🍎 ' , ' 🍒 ' , ' 🍌 ' ]; fruits . slice ( 2 ); // ["🍒", "🍌"]

如果此数组中至少有一个元素满足提供的测试函数，则返回true 。

 // 15. some() const fruits = [ ' 🍉 ' , ' 🍎 ' , ' 🍒 ' , ' 🍌 ' ]; const bananaExists = fruits . some ( fruit => fruit === ' 🍌 ' )); // true

对数组的元素进行就地排序并返回该数组。

 // 16. sort() const fruits = [ ' 🍉 ' , ' 🍎 ' , ' 🍒 ' , ' 🍌 ' , ' 🍉 ' , ' 🍉 ' , ' 🍒 ' , ' 🍎 ' , ' 🍌 ' ]; fruits . sort (); // ["🍉", "🍉", "🍉", "🍌", "🍌", "🍎", "🍎", "🍒", "🍒"]

从数组中添加和/或删除元素。

 // 17. splice() const fruits = [ ' 🍉 ' , ' 🍎 ' , ' 🍒 ' , ' 🍌 ' ]; fruits . splice ( 2 , 1 , ' 🥝 ' ); // ["🍉", "🍎", "🥝", "🍌"]

如果你喜欢这篇文章，一定要❤️它。

快乐编码！ 如果您有任何问题，可以在Twitter 上与我联系。

您可以在我的博客上找到大量现实生活中的技巧和资源。

古拉夫·辛格·拉瓦特—— May 22 5月22日



原文:

Marko Denic

Posted on May 25

Returns a new array that is this array joined with other array(s) and/or value(s).

const fruits = ['🍉', '🍎', '🍒', '🍌'];
const vegetables = ['🧅', '🌽', '🥕', '🥑']; // 1. concat()
const food = fruits.concat(vegetables); // ["🍉", "🍎", "🍒", "🍌", "🧅", "🌽", "🥕", "🥑"]

2. copyWithin()

Copies a sequence of array elements within the array.

// 2. copyWithin()
const fruits = ['🍉', '🍎', '🍒', '🍌']; const fruitsCopy = fruits.copyWithin(0, 2); // ["🍒", "🍌", "🍒", "🍌"]

3. every()

Returns true if every element in this array satisfies the testing function.

// 3. every()
const fruits = ['🍉', '🍎', '🍒', '🍌']; const allBananas = fruits.every(fruit => fruit === '🍌'); // false

4. fill()

Fills all the elements of an array from a start index to an end index with a static value.

// 4. fill()
const fruits = ['🍉', '🍎', '🍒', '🍌']; const lemons = fruits.fill('🍋'); // ["🍋", "🍋", "🍋", "🍋"]

5. filter()

Returns a new array containing all elements of the calling array for which the provided filtering function returns true.

// 5. filter()
const fruits = ['🍉', '🍎', '🍒', '🍌']; const onlyBananas = ['🍉', '🍎', '🍌', '🍌'].filter(fruit => fruit === '🍌'); // ["🍌", "🍌"]

6. find()

Returns the found element in the array, if some element in the array satisfies the testing function, or undefined if not found.

// 6. find()
const fruits = ['🍉', '🍎', '🍒', '🍌']; const cherry = fruits.find(fruit => fruit === '🍒'); // "🍒"

7. findIndex()

Returns the found index in the array, if an element in the array satisfies the testing function, or -1 if not found.

// 7. findIndex()
const fruits = ['🍉', '🍎', '🍒', '🍌']; const cherryIndex = fruits.findIndex(fruit => fruit === '🍒'); // 2

8. forEach()

Calls a function for each element in the array.

// 8. forEach()
const vegetables = ['🧅', '🌽', '🥕', '🥑']; vegetables.forEach(vegetable => console.log(vegetable));
// "🥦"
// "🌽"
// "🥕"
// "🥑"

9. includes()

Determines whether the array contains a value, returning true or false as appropriate.

// 9. includes()
const vegetables = ['🧅', '🌽', '🥕', '🥑']; const includesCorn = vegetables.includes('🌽'); // true
const includesTomato = vegetables.includes('🍅'); // false

10. join()

Joins all elements of an array into a string.

// 10. join()
const vegetables = ['🧅', '🌽', '🥕', '🥑']; const vegetablesGroup = vegetables.join(''); // "🥦🌽🥕🥑"

11. map()

Returns a new array containing the results of calling a function on every element in this array.

// 11. map()
const vegetables = ['🧅', '🌽', '🥕', '🥑']; const doubledVegetables = vegetables.map(vegetable => vegetable + vegetable); // ["🥦🥦", "🌽🌽", "🥕🥕", "🥑🥑"]

12. push()

Adds one or more elements to the end of an array, and returns the new length of the array.

// 12. push()
const fruits = ['🍉', '🍎', '🍒', '🍌']; fruits.push('🥝'); // ["🍉", "🍎", "🍒", "🍌", "🥝"]

13. reverse()

Reverses the order of the elements of an array in place.

// 13. reverse()
const fruits = ['🍉', '🍎', '🍒', '🍌']; const fruits = ['🍉', '🍎', '🍒', '🍌']; const reversedFruits = fruits.reverse(); // ["🍌", "🍒", "🍎", "🍉"]

14. slice()

Extracts a section of the calling array and returns a new array.

// 14. slice()
const fruits = ['🍉', '🍎', '🍒', '🍌']; fruits.slice(2); // ["🍒", "🍌"]

15. some()

Returns true if at least one element in this array satisfies the provided testing function.

// 15. some()
const fruits = ['🍉', '🍎', '🍒', '🍌']; const bananaExists = fruits.some(fruit => fruit === '🍌')); // true

16. sort()

Sorts the elements of an array in place and returns the array.

// 16. sort()
const fruits = ['🍉', '🍎', '🍒', '🍌', '🍉', '🍉', '🍒', '🍎', '🍌'];
fruits.sort(); // ["🍉", "🍉", "🍉", "🍌", "🍌", "🍎", "🍎", "🍒", "🍒"]

17. splice()

Adds and/or removes elements from an array.

// 17. splice()
const fruits = ['🍉', '🍎', '🍒', '🍌']; fruits.splice(2, 1, '🥝'); // ["🍉", "🍎", "🥝", "🍌"]

If you liked this article, be sure to ❤️ it.

Happy coding! ❤️

If you have any questions, you can contact me on Twitter.

You can find a ton of real-life tips and resources on my blog.

Gourav Singh Rawat - May 22
Items.Author: denicmarko

Items.Title: 7 个热门 🔥 功能，让您开始使用 dart ⏭️ | 7 Hot 🔥 features that will get you started with dart ⏭️
Items.Description:
大家好，在这个系列中，这是开始扑腾之前了解飞镖的第三章，看看：

一旦分配了一个值，最终变量的值就不能更改。 最终值只能在运行时确定。 因此就像使用DateTime.now()时一样，因为当前日期和时间只能是
在运行时确定，而不是在编译时确定。

  • 您可以在创建集合时使用它，例如 const [1, 2, 3]，以及在构造对象（而不是 new）时，例如 const Point(2, 3)。 在这里，const 意味着对象的整个深度状态可以完全在编译时确定，并且对象将被冻结并且完全不可变。

  • 然后 for var 是完全可变的，因为它的值可以在任何时候重新分配。 这些变量的任何内容都不能更改一次
    他们被分配了。

    • 它们是使用不可变数据类型的两个主要好处，它们是：

    • 它更快。 当你声明一个 const 值时，编译器要做的工作就更少了。 它只需为该变量分配一次内存，并且无需担心重新分配变量时的重新分配。 这似乎是一个微不足道的增益，但随着程序的增长，您的性能增益也会增长。
    • 不可变数据没有副作用。 编程中最常见的错误来源之一是值在一个地方发生更改，它会导致意外的级联更改。 如果数据不能改变，那么就不会有级联。 在实践中，大多数变量往往只分配一次，那么为什么不利用不变性呢？

    可能值得一提的是执行连接任务的另一种方法，即使用StringBuffer对象。 考虑以下代码：

     List fruits = ['Strawberry', 'Coconut', 'Orange', 'Mango', 'Apple']; StringBuffer buffer = StringBuffer(); for (String fruit in fruits) { buffer.write(fruit); buffer.write(' '); } print (buffer.toString()); // prints: Strawberry Coconut Orange Mango Apple

    因为您可以使用 StringBuffer 来增量构建字符串。 这比使用字符串连接要好，因为它性能更好。 您可以通过调用其 write 方法将内容添加到 StringBuffer。 然后，一旦它被创建，你可以将它转换成一个字符串

    参数后面的问号，比如String? 请记住，我们在上一章中介绍了这一点😝，所以在这里它们是这样使用的：

     void unnamed([String? name, int? age]) { final actualName = name ?? 'Unknown'; final actualAge = age ?? 0; print('$actualName is $actualAge years old.'); }

    要添加的另一件事还支持带有花括号的命名可选参数。 那么在调用带有命名参数的函数时，需要指定参数名称。 可以任意顺序调用参数； 为了

    例如， named(greeting: 'hello!') 而且 Dart 还支持参数的默认值。

    闭包，也称为一等函数，是 1930 年代从 lambda 演算中出现的一个有趣的语言特性。 基本思想是函数也是一个值，可以作为参数传递给其他函数。 这些类型的函数称为闭包，但函数和闭包之间实际上没有区别。 闭包可以保存到变量中并用作其他函数的参数。 当使用期望闭包作为属性的函数时，它们甚至是内联编写的。 我们可以使用 typedef 关键字优化闭包的使用看看：

     typedef NumberGetter = int Function(); int powerOfTwo(NumberGetter getter) { return getter() * getter(); }

    现在，当您熟悉其他语言时，您知道每种语言都有一种组织数据的方法，您可以使用以下集合之一：

    • 列表是保持元素顺序的线性集合。
    • 映射是可以通过唯一键访问的值的非线性集合。
    • 集合是不维护顺序的唯一值的非线性集合如果 Dart 不是您的第一种编程语言，那么这个矩阵应该可以帮助您将集合与其他语言中的等效概念相关联：

    这些集合的一个共同点是下标语法。 下标是一种快速访问集合中元素的方法，并且它们在不同语言中的工作方式往往相同。 喜欢：

    万岁！！！！ 谢谢😉，请继续关注更多文章🎉
    如需进一步解释，请前往 dart.dev。



    原文:

    Hello folks, In this series, this is the 3rd chapter of what to know about dart before getting started to flutter have a look :

    1. Difference between const, final and var 🤔

    • Final means single-assignment: a final variable or field must have an initializer. Once assigned a value, a final variable's value cannot be changed. final modifies variables. Final value can only be determined at run time. Thus like when using DateTime.now() since the current date and time can only be
      determined at runtime, not at compile time.

    • Const has a meaning that's a bit more complex 😯 and subtle in Dart. const modifies values. You can use it when creating collections, like const [1, 2, 3], and when constructing objects (instead of new) like const Point(2, 3). Here, const means that the object's entire deep state can be determined entirely at compile time and that the object will be frozen and completely immutable.

    • Then for var is completely mutable as its value can be reassigned at any point. final variables can only be assigned once, but by using objects, you can change the value of its fields. const variables are compile-time constants and are fully immutable; nothing about these variables can be changed once
      they've been assigned.

    2.Why should we use immutable data types ?

    They are two primary benefits of using immutable data types which are:

    • It's faster. When you declare a const value, the compiler has less work to do. It only has to allocate memory for that variable once and doesn't need to worry about reallocating if the variable is reassigned. This may seem like an infinitesimal gain, but as your programs grow, your performance gain grows as well.
    • Immutable data does not have side effects. One of the most common sources of bugs in programming is where value is changed in one place, and it causes an unexpected cascade of changes. If the data cannot change, then there will be no cascade. And in practice, most variables tend to only be assigned once anyway, so why not take advantage of immutability?

    3. String buffer in dart

    The first interesting thing about strings in dart is that it's okay if you use single quotes or double quotes they both work 😝
    It's probably worth mentioning another way to perform concatenation tasks, which is using the StringBufferobject. Consider the following code:

    List fruits = ['Strawberry', 'Coconut', 'Orange', 'Mango', 'Apple'];
StringBuffer buffer = StringBuffer();
for (String fruit in fruits) {
 buffer.write(fruit);
 buffer.write(' ');
}
print (buffer.toString()); // prints: Strawberry Coconut Orange Mango Apple

    As you can use a StringBuffer to incrementally build a string. This is better than using string concatenation as it performs better. You add content to a StringBuffer by calling its write method. Then, once it's been created, you can transform it into a String with the

    toString method.

    4. Types of parameters in dart
    Dart has two types of parameters which are > named and positional parameters.

    **Positional optional parameters **are those parameters that are used if you wrap your function's parameter list in square brackets [ ], then those parameters can be omitted without the compiler throwing errors. The question mark after a parameter, such as in String? name, tells the Dart compiler that the parameter itself can be null. Remember we covered this in the previous chapter 😝 so here by they are used like this:

    void unnamed([String? name, int? age]) {
 final actualName = name ?? 'Unknown';
 final actualAge = age ?? 0;
 print('$actualName is $actualAge years old.');
}

    One more thing to add also supports named optional parameters with curly braces. Then When calling a function with named parameters, you need to specify the parameter name. You can call the parameters in any order; for

    example, named(greeting: 'hello!');. And Dart also supports default values for parameters.

    5. Closures

    Closures, also known as first-class functions, are an interesting language feature that emerged from lambda calculus in the 1930s. The basic idea is that a function is also a value that can be passed around to other functions as a parameter. These types of functions are called closures, but there is really no difference between a function and a closure. Closures can be saved to variables and used as parameters for other functions. They are even written inline when consuming a function that expects a closure as a property. We can optimise the use of closures using typedef keyword have a look:

    typedef NumberGetter = int Function();
int powerOfTwo(NumberGetter getter) {
 return getter() * getter();
}

    6. Relationships between classes in dart

    7. Grouping data and manipulating data in dart

    Now as you are familiar with other languages you know that each language has a way to organize data, you can use one of these collections:

    • Lists are linear collections where the order of the elements is maintained.
    • Maps are a non-linear collection of values that can be accessed by a unique key.
    • Sets are a non-linear collection of unique values where the order is not maintained If Dart is not your first programming language, then this matrix should help you correlate collections to equivalent concepts in other languages:

    One thing that these collections have in common is a subscript syntax. Subscripts are a way to quickly access elements in a collection, and they tend to work identically from language to language. like:

    Horrayyy!!!! ✈️ Thanks fo reading to this far now you're good to go with dart this marks the end of our series about the introduction to dart. Thank you 😉, stay tuned for more articles 🎉
    And for further explanation please head to dart.dev.

Items.Title: 创建一个自定义的 React 秒表计时器组件 | Create a custom React stopwatch timer component
Items.Description:
在本教程中，我们将创建一个 React 秒表计时器组件。 完成的秒表组件将显示分钟、秒和毫秒，并具有启动、停止和重置功能。

让我们开始使用Create React App设置开发环境：

npx create-react-app react-stopwatch

接下来在/src文件夹中创建一个新的Stopwatch.js文件：

 import React , { useState , useEffect } from " react " ; const Stopwatch = () => { //... }; export default Stopwatch ;

我们将使用两个 React Hooks，首先是useState ，它允许我们将状态存储在基于函数的组件中。 它将用于存储秒表时间以及秒表是否正在运行。 在Stopwatch()函数中，我们将首先声明time和running状态的变量：

 const Stopwatch = () => { const [ time , setTime ] = useState ( 0 ); const [ running , setRunning ] = useState ( false ); //... };

然后我们将使用useEffect() Hook & setInterval()方法计算时间：

 const Stopwatch = () => { const [ time , setTime ] = useState ( 0 ); const [ running , setRunning ] = useState ( false ); useEffect (() => { let interval ; if ( running ) { interval = setInterval (() => { setTime (( prevTime ) => prevTime + 10 ); }, 10 ); } else if ( ! running ) { clearInterval ( interval ); } return () => clearInterval ( interval ); }, [ running ]); //... };

为了完成组件，我们将输出时间以及控制按钮：

 const Stopwatch = () => { const [ time , setTime ] = useState ( 0 ); const [ running , setRunning ] = useState ( false ); useEffect (() => { let interval ; if ( running ) { interval = setInterval (() => { setTime (( prevTime ) => prevTime + 10 ); }, 10 ); } else if ( ! running ) { clearInterval ( interval ); } return () => clearInterval ( interval ); }, [ running ]); return ( < div className = " stopwatch " > < div className = " numbers " > < span > {( " 0 " + Math . floor (( time / 60000 ) % 60 )). slice ( - 2 )}: < /span > < span > {( " 0 " + Math . floor (( time / 1000 ) % 60 )). slice ( - 2 )}: < /span > < span > {( " 0 " + (( time / 10 ) % 100 )). slice ( - 2 )} < /span > < /div > < div className = " buttons " > < button onClick = {() => setRunning ( true )} > Start < /button > < button onClick = {() => setRunning ( false )} > Stop < /button > < button onClick = {() => setTime ( 0 )} > Reset < /button> < /div > < /div > ); };

时间是通过将时间除以每个时间单位的毫秒数来计算的。 然后我们使用余数运算符%来计算时间是否可以被 60 秒、60 分钟和 100 毫秒整除。 例如 1 分 20 秒是 80000 毫秒，因此计算我们所做的秒数(80000 / 1000) % 60 = 20 。 如果没有余数运算符(80000 / 1000) = 80 ，我们只会得到总秒数。

最后替换App.js的内容来导入和加载秒表组件：

 import Stopwatch from " ./Stopwatch " ; const App = () => { return ( < div className = " App " > < Stopwatch /> < /div > ); }; export default App ;

这就是本教程的全部内容，希望它可以作为您构建更复杂的基于时间的应用程序的起点。 当你在这里时，一定要查看我们不断扩大的实用 React 教程集合。



原文:

In this tutorial we’ll be creating a React stopwatch timer component. The completed stopwatch component will display minutes, seconds, and milliseconds with start, stop, and reset functionality.

Let’s get started by setting up a dev environment using Create React App:

npx create-react-app react-stopwatch

Next create a new Stopwatch.js file in the /src folder:

import React, { useState, useEffect } from "react"; const Stopwatch = () => { //...
}; export default Stopwatch;

We’ll be using two React Hooks, firstly useState which allows us to store state in a function based component. It’ll be used to store the stopwatch time and also whether or not the stopwatch is running. The useEffect Hook checks if the timer is running and if so updates the time.

In the Stopwatch() function we’ll first declare variables for the time and running states:

const Stopwatch = () => { const [time, setTime] = useState(0); const [running, setRunning] = useState(false); //...
};

Then we’ll calculate the time using a useEffect() Hook & setInterval() method:

const Stopwatch = () => { const [time, setTime] = useState(0); const [running, setRunning] = useState(false); useEffect(() => { let interval; if (running) { interval = setInterval(() => { setTime((prevTime) => prevTime + 10); }, 10); } else if (!running) { clearInterval(interval); } return () => clearInterval(interval); }, [running]); //...
};

To complete the component we’ll output the time along with control buttons:

const Stopwatch = () => { const [time, setTime] = useState(0); const [running, setRunning] = useState(false); useEffect(() => { let interval; if (running) { interval = setInterval(() => { setTime((prevTime) => prevTime + 10); }, 10); } else if (!running) { clearInterval(interval); } return () => clearInterval(interval); }, [running]); return ( <div className="stopwatch"> <div className="numbers"> <span>{("0" + Math.floor((time / 60000) % 60)).slice(-2)}:</span>
 <span>{("0" + Math.floor((time / 1000) % 60)).slice(-2)}:</span>
 <span>{("0" + ((time / 10) % 100)).slice(-2)}</span>
 </div>
 <div className="buttons"> <button onClick={() => setRunning(true)}>Start</button>
 <button onClick={() => setRunning(false)}>Stop</button>
 <button onClick={() => setTime(0)}>Reset</button>   </div>
 </div>
 );
};

The time is calculated by dividing the time by the number of milliseconds for each unit of time. We then use the remainder operator % to calculate if the time is divisible by 60 for seconds, 60 for minutes and, 100 for milliseconds. For example 1 minute 20 seconds is 80000 milliseconds so to calculate the seconds we do (80000 / 1000) % 60 = 20. Without the remainder operator (80000 / 1000) = 80 we just get the total seconds.

Finally replace the contents of App.js to import and load the stopwatch component:

import Stopwatch from "./Stopwatch"; const App = () => { return ( <div className="App"> <Stopwatch /> </div>
 );
}; export default App;

That’s all for this tutorial, hopefully it’ll serve as a starting point for you to build more complex time based applications. Whilst you’re here be sure to checkout our ever expanding collection of practical React tutorials.

Items.Title: 图和图的类型🔥 | Graphs and Types of Graphs🔥
Items.Description:
丽蒂卡·坎杜里

发表于希望你会喜欢✨

因此，让我们从主题开始：-图表
什么是 Graph，为什么知道它很重要？🤔

对于那些准备实习和编码面试的人来说，图表是最重要的话题之一，因为大多数公司在编码轮次中都会提出图表问题。

让我们从Graphs的定义开始

图是由一组边连接的有限顶点（即节点）组成的非线性数据结构。
 边是连接两个顶点的线。 它由G = (V,E)定义

你知道 DSA 中不同类型的图表吗？ 如果是，那就太好了。 继续修改概念✌️

  • 如果不是，让我们了解这一点🔥

    • 图表类型

    • 有限图- 顾名思义 (Finite) ，它是具有有限数量的顶点和边的图。

    • 无限图- 它是具有无限顶点和边的图。

    • 平凡图- 如果一个有限图只有一个顶点，它就是平凡图。 它没有任何优势。

    • 简单图- 如果在任何一对顶点之间存在一条且仅一条边，则该图是该图。

    • 多图- 它是在任意两个顶点之间包含多条边或环的图。

    • 空图- 这是具有 n 个孤立顶点但它们之间没有连接边的平凡图的情况。

    • 具有 n 个顶点的无向完全图将具有 n(n-1)/2 条边

    • 具有 n 个顶点的有向完全图将具有 n(n-1) 条边

      • 伪图- 它是包含自环和其他边的图。

      • 正则图- 它是每个顶点具有相同度数的简单图。 因此，每个完全图都是正则图。

      • 二分图- 如果一个图的顶点可以划分为两个非空的不相交集，则该图称为二分图。 在二分图中，将没有连接来自同一集合的顶点的边。

      • 它也被称为加权图

      • 边缘由箭头表示。箭头表示其方向。 例如 A->B 意味着从 A 到 B 有一条边，反之亦然。

      • 子图- 它是一个图，其顶点和边是另一个图的子集。

      两种类型的子图：-

      1. 顶点不相交子图：- 没有公共顶点的子图。

      2. 边缘不相交子图：- 没有共同边的子图。

      • 连通图或不连通图- 如果存在从图的一个顶点到图的任何其他顶点的路径，则该图称为连通图。 它至少有 n-1 条边。

      如果未连接，则该图称为断开连接。

      • 循环图- 具有一个或多个循环的图称为循环图。 它包含一条从至少一个节点返回到自身的路径。

      • 顶点标记- 它是在其顶点中保存数据的图。 它可以帮助确定边缘数据，例如（键，值）对映射。

      • 有向无环图- 如果边是有向的且不包含循环，则该图称为 DAG。边使用有序的顶点对表示。

      HURRAY🥳. You have learnt all the types of graph 🙌.

      Thanks for your patience and I hope you enjoy reading this blog .
      Kindly share your feedback , it will motivate me to write more such blogs🙂.

    伊曼纽尔·巴托莱西 - May 21 5月21日



    原文:

    Ritika Khanduri

    Posted on May 25

    Hello Everyone👋. It is my first time blogging. I love to write about programming and development. Here is my LinkedIn Profile. Let's Connect https://www.linkedin.com/in/ritika-khanduri/

    Hope you will like it✨

    So Let's Start the Journey with the Topic : - GRAPHS
    What is Graph and Why is it important to know it?🤔

    The graph is the one of the most important topics for those preparing for placements and coding interviews as most companies ask graph questions during the coding rounds.

    Let's start with definition of Graphs

    Graphs are non-linear data structures consisting of a finite set of vertices (i.e. nodes) connected by a set of edges.
    An edge is the line which connects two vertices . It is defined by G = (V,E)

    Do you know different types of graph in DSA ?? 🤔.

    • If Yes, that's great. Continue to Revise the concept ✌️

    • If No, Let's Understand this 🔥

    Types of Graphs

    • Finite Graph - As the name suggest (Finite) , it is the graph with finite number of vertices and edges.

    • Infinite Graph - It is the graph with infinite vertices and edges.

    • Trivial Graph - A finite graph is trivial graph if it has only one vertex. It does not have any edge.

    • Simple Graph - It is the graph if there exist one and only one edge between any pair of vertices.

    • Multi Graph - It is the graph which contains more than one edge or loop between any two vertices.

    • Null Graph - It is the case of trivial graph having n isolated vertices but no connecting edge between them.

    • Complete Graph - It is the simple undirected graph in which every pair of distinct vertices is connected by a unique edge.It is also known as a full graph, and the degree of each vertex must be n-1.Every pair of vertices should be connected.

    1. An undirected complete graph with n vertices will have n(n-1)/2 edges

    2. A directed complete graph with n vertices will have n(n-1) edges

    • Pseudo Graph - It is the graph containing self loop along with other edges.

    • Regular Graph - It is the simple graph with each vertex having the same degree . Therefore , every complete graph is regular graph.

    • Bipartite Graph - A graph is called bipartite graph if its vertices can be partitioned in two non empty disjoint sets. In bipartite graphs, there would be no edge that connects vertices from the same set.

    • Labelled Graph - The graph is labelled graph if the edges are labelled with the name, data or weight . It is also known as weighted graph

    • Digraph - It is the directed graph in which vertices are ordered in particular sequence. The edges are represented by arrows .The arrow shows its direction. For example A->B means there is an edge from A to B not vice versa.

    • Subgraph - It is a graph whose vertices and edges are subsets of another graph.

    Two Types of Subgraph :-

    1. Vertices Disjoint Subgraph :- A subgraph with no common vertex.

    2. Edge Disjoint Subgraph :- A subgraph with no common edge.

    • Connected or Disconnected Graph - The graph is called connected if there exist a path from one vertex of graph to any other vertex of graph. It has atleast n-1 edges.

    The graph is called disconnected if it is not connected.

    • Cyclic Graph - A graph that has one or more cycles is called a cyclic graph. It contains a path from at least one node back to itself.

    • Vertex labelled - It is the graph that holds data in its vertices. It can help to determine the edges data like (key, value) pair mapping.

    • Directed Acyclic Graph - The graph is called DAG if the edges are directed and doesn't contain cycle.The edges are represented using an ordered pair of vertices.

    HURRAY🥳. You have learnt all the types of graph 🙌.

    Thanks for your patience and I hope you enjoy reading this blog .
    Kindly share your feedback , it will motivate me to write more such blogs🙂.

    Adheeban Manoharan - May 21

    Emanuele Bartolesi - May 21

Items.Title: A11Y 101：具有多个链接的可访问卡片 | A11Y 101: Accessible cards with multiple links
Items.Description:
在审核我的网站时，我注意到我的卡片上有一个奇怪的双标签行为。

这是卡片设计中常见的可访问性问题。
为了说明，让我们拿一张我的卡片。

在图像中，我们可以看到整张卡片。 在卡片内部，它以带有链接的图像开头。 下面是一个标题，其中还包含一个链接。

在此之下，我们有一些不一定是链接的通用帖子信息。

可访问性问题

问题出现在两个链接上。
首先，它们都导航到同一个东西，所以重复它有点多余。

其次，想象图像有其他一些不一定解释链接的描述性替代文本？

屏幕阅读器将读出该图像的替代文本，用户可能希望去其他地方。

所以，我们需要解决这个问题。
坦率地说，有多种方法可以做到这一点。

使用标签索引

这样，您保留了两个链接，但您可以将图像的tab index设置为-1 。

为了使其更加健壮，我们可以在此链接中添加一个 aria-hidden 以确保它被忽略。

 <a href= "/post" tabindex= "-1" aria-hidden= "true" > <img src= "img.jpg" alt= "An image of Remix.run blog article" /> </a>

由于此选项卡，索引屏幕阅读器将在其上导航。

这是迄今为止最简单的方法，在这种情况下，屏幕阅读器仍然能够阅读您的图像。

或者，您可以选择通过使用 CSS 隐藏整个链接来使整个链接不可见。 然后我们可以将它绝对定位在图像上。

包装卡片

另一种选择是包装整张卡片并使用描述性的 aria-label 告诉用户发生了什么。

就我而言，我只希望图像和标题是可链接的，您可能想知道，但您肯定已经可以做到了吗？

在桌面上，我可以，但如果我们查看我的移动视图，你会发现文本实际上介于两者之间。
要包装整个卡片，我们可以通过在内容周围使用<a href>或添加位于顶部的绝对位置链接来原生覆盖它。

我更喜欢语义方式，所以它看起来像这样：

 <a href= "/post" > <h3> Title of post </h3> <img src= "img.jpg" alt= "An image of Remix.run blog article" /> </a>

但是，屏幕阅读器现在将阅读“链接：帖子标题 Remix.run 博客文章的图像”。

如果我们在卡片中包含更多副本，也许会更多。
这并不理想，因为我们想控制它读取的内容。

我们可以通过在链接中添加一个 aria 标签来解决这个问题。

 <a href= "/post" aria-label= "Title of post" > <h3> Title of post </h3> <img src= "img.jpg" alt= "An image of Remix.run blog article" /> </a>

屏幕阅读器只会显示：“链接：帖子标题”。

结论

从 SEO 的角度来看，重复链接也可能会给您带来更多问题，因此您始终可以尝试并选择单链接方法。

但是，如果您因为多个不同的链接而无法访问，您可以选择对屏幕阅读器隐藏某些链接。

明智地选择你的战斗，不要忘记语义 HTML 总是赢！

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系



原文:

While auditing my website, I noticed a weird double tab behavior on my cards.

This is a common accessibility issue in card designs.
To illustrate, let's take one of my cards.

In the image, we can see the whole card. Inside the card, it starts with an image with a link. And below that is a title, which also contains a link.

Under that, we have some generic post information that doesn't have to be a link.

The accessibility issue

The issue arises with the two links.
First of all, they both navigate to the same thing, so it's a bit redundant to repeat itself.

Secondly, imagine the image having some other descriptive alt text that doesn't necessarily explain the link?

The screen reader will read out that image alt-text, and the user might expect to go somewhere else.

So, we need to address this issue.
And frankly, there are multiple ways of doing this.

Using tab index

In this way, you keep the two links, but you can set the tab index of the image to -1.

To make it even more robust, we can add an aria-hidden to this link to ensure it's ignored.

<a href="/post" tabindex="-1" aria-hidden="true"> <img src="img.jpg" alt="An image of Remix.run blog article" />
</a>

Because of this tab, index screen readers will navigate over it.

This is by far the easiest way, and screen readers will still be able to read your image in this case.

Alternatively, you could opt to make the whole link invisible by using CSS to hide it. We could then position it absolutely over the image.

Wrapping the card

Another option is to wrap the whole card and use a descriptive aria-label to tell the user what's happening.

In my case, I only want the image and title to be linkable, and you might be wondering, but surely you can already do that?

And on desktop, I could, but if we look at my mobile view, you can see the text is actually in between the two.
(This is also why the tab first goes on the title and only then on the image).

To wrap the whole card, we can cover it natively by using the <a href> around the content or adding an absolute positions link that sits on top.

I prefer the semantic way so it could look like this:

<a href="/post"> <h3>Title of post</h3> <img src="img.jpg" alt="An image of Remix.run blog article" />
</a>

However, screen readers will now read "Link: Title of post An image of Remix.run blog article".

And perhaps even more if we include more copies in the card.
This is not ideal as we want to control what it reads.

We can fix this by adding an aria label to the link.

<a href="/post" aria-label="Title of post"> <h3>Title of post</h3> <img src="img.jpg" alt="An image of Remix.run blog article" />
</a>

The screen readers will only read: "Link: Title of post".

Conclusion

The duplicate links might give you more issues also from an SEO perspective, so you can always try and opt for the single link approach.

However, if you can't because of multiple different links, you can opt to hide certain links from screen readers.

Pick your battles wisely, and don't forget semantic HTML always wins!

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter

Items.Title: 使用 M1 机器，Flutter 构建速度更快：VM 的比较 | Flutter builds are way faster with M1 machines: A comparison of VMs
Items.Description:
对于 iOS 构建（带有缓存），与 Mac Pro 相比，我们发现构建时间缩短了约 50% 。

从那时起，我们都阅读并从其他人那里听到了有关疯狂性能改进的信息。 也许您已经阅读了 Jameson Williams（Reddit 的一名软件工程师）的推文：

所以我们知道 M1 机器很快，但是我们应该在 CI/CD 中使用它们来加速我们的管道吗？ 为了帮助回答这个问题，我们将适用于 iOS 和 macOS 的 M1 mini、Mac Pro 和 Mac mini与 Codemagic 进行了比较。 在本文中，我们将为您提供决定升级是否值得所需的事实和数据。

本文由Nils Reichardt撰写，最初发布到Codemagic 博客

比较设置

让我们谈谈这个比较的设置，并看看以下问题：

  • 哪些应用程序已用于构建？
  • 哪些平台？
  • 发布、调试或分析模式？

应用

为了比较 Flutter 应用程序的构建，我们使用了两个应用程序：

  • 在比较中，我们更关注 Sharezone 应用程序，因为 Sharezone 是一个真正的生产应用程序，拥有+300,000 注册用户。 它使用了一堆依赖项，就像许多Firebase包和其他几个包一样（Sharezone 是开源的——你可以在这里找到所有依赖项）。 因此，您将看到非常真实的构建时间。

    平台

    我们专注于以下方面：

    • 苹果系统
    • 测试（单元和小部件测试）

    系统

    我们正在使用 Codemagic 的虚拟机进行测试，您可以将其用于 CI/CD。 这些机器具有以下配置：

    • 颤振配置

      我们使用的 Flutter 版本是 2.10.5

      为了获得更真实的视图，所有构建都使用了 Flutter 的发布模式。

      对于测试，我们只使用了flutter test命令。

      依赖缓存

      我们比较了没有缓存和有缓存的构建。 对于缓存，我们缓存了以下路径：

      • 有关如何在 Codemagic 中使用缓存的更多信息，请查看有关依赖缓存的文档。

        准确性

        我们每次构建运行 3 次并计算平均值。 例如，iOS Counter 应用程序的后期构建时间是三个构建的平均时间。

        我们总共运行了54 次构建。 您还可以查看此电子表格，其中显示了所有原始数据和构建的链接。

        比较 M1 Mini、Mac Pro 和 Mac Mini 的结果

        我们来到本文的中心部分：结果。 您将看到 Counter 和 Sharezone 应用程序在构建时间方面的差异有多大。 因此，必须使用真实的应用程序进行此类比较。

        所有构建时间都以秒为单位。

        计数器应用结果

        平台麦克迷你苹果系统缓存麦克迷你不是的结果令人难以置信！ 与 Mac mini 相比，M1 机器减少了68%的构建时间（使用缓存），与 Mac Pro 相比减少了53% 。

        苹果系统

        缓存麦克迷你不是的在 macOS 上，与 iOS 构建相比，我们没有看到巨大的改进，但 M1 构建（带有缓存）仍然比 Mac mini 构建快 34%，比 Mac Pro 构建快 17%。

        单元和小部件测试

        麦克迷你在单元测试和小部件测试方面比较机器时，我们发现它们之间几乎没有区别。

        旁注：Sharezone 有 238 个单元和小部件测试。

        总体

        概括

        总结一下结果： M1 机器比 Mac Pro 机器快了近 50% ，在集成测试方面，它们的结果特别有趣。 我们都知道缓慢的 CI 管道很糟糕。 但是，M1 机器不仅会减少您的集成测试的构建时间，而且如果您想在 App Store 或 Play Store 上发布您的应用程序，它们也是您的必经之路。

        开始使用 Codemagic 和 M1 虚拟机！



        原文:

        TL;DR: M1 machines are astonishingly fast! For iOS builds (with caching), we see build time improvements of ~50% compared with the Mac Pros.

        In November 2020, Apple released the M1 MacBook and, one year later, the M1 Pro/Max chips. Since then, we have all read and heard from other people about the crazy performance improvements. Perhaps you already read the tweet by Jameson Williams (a staff software engineer at Reddit):

        So we know that M1 machines are fast, but should we use them in CI/CD to speed up our pipeline? To help answer that question, we compared the M1 mini, Mac Pro, and Mac mini for iOS and macOS builds with Codemagic. In this article, we’ll give you the facts and figures you need to decide if an upgrade is worth it.

        This article is written by Nils Reichardt and originally posted to Codemagic blog

        Comparison setup

        Let’s talk about the setup of this comparison and take a look at questions like:

        • Which apps have been used for the builds?
        • Which platforms?
        • Release, debug, or profiling mode?

        Apps

        To compare the builds of the Flutter apps, we used two apps:

        • The default Counter app of Flutter
        • Sharezone — an open-source school planner students, parents, and teachers can use to organize together

        In the comparison, we focus more on the Sharezone app because Sharezone is a real production app with +300,000 registered users. It uses a bunch of dependencies, like many of the Firebase packages and several other ones (Sharezone is open source — you can find all the dependencies here). Therefore, you will see very realistic build times.

        Platforms

        We are focusing on the following:

        • iOS
        • macOS
        • Tests (unit and widget tests)

        System

        We are using Codemagic’s virtual machines for the test, which you can use for CI/CD. These machines have the following configuration:

        • Mac Mini: 2,3GHz Quad Core / 8 GB
        • Mac Pro: 3.7GHz Quad Core / 32 GB
        • M1 Mini: 3.2 Quad Core / 8 GB

        Flutter Configuration

        Flutter version that we used is 2.10.5

        To get a more realistic view, all builds used the release mode of Flutter.

        For the tests, we just used the flutter test command.

        Dependency caching

        We compared the builds without and with caching. For caching, we cached the following paths:

        • Dart cache: $FLUTTER_ROOT/.pub-cache
        • CocoaPods cache: HOME/Library/Caches/CocoaPods

        For more information about how to use caching in Codemagic, just take a look at the documentation on dependency caching.

        Accuracy

        We ran every build three times and calculated the average. For example, the later build time for the iOS Counter app is the average time of three builds.

        In total, we ran 54 builds. You can also take a look at this spreadsheet, which shows all the raw data and links to the builds.

        Results of comparing M1 Mini vs Mac Pro vs Mac Mini

        We are coming to the central part of this article: the results. You will see how big the difference is in terms of build time between the Counter and the Sharezone apps. Therefore, it’s essential to use a real app for comparisons like this.

        All build times are measured in seconds.

        Counter app results

        Platform Mac mini Mac Pro M1 mini
        iOS 92 75 30
        macOS 89 51 25

        Sharezone app results

        iOS

        Caching Mac mini Mac Pro M1 mini
        No 967 658 395
        Yes 967 658 395

        The results are incredible! The M1 machines reduce build times (with caching) by 68% compared with the Mac mini and 53% compared with the Mac Pro.

        macOS

        Caching Mac mini Mac Pro M1 mini
        No 479 466 433
        Yes 501 401 333

        On macOS, we don’t see as huge of improvements compared with the iOS builds, but the M1 builds (with caching) are still 34% faster than the Mac mini builds and 17% faster than the Mac Pro builds.

        Unit and widget tests

        Mac mini Mac Pro M1 mini
        84 90 95

        When comparing the machines in terms of unit and widget tests, we see nearly no differences between them.

        Side note: Sharezone has 238 unit and widget tests.

        Overall

        Summary

        To sum up the results: The M1 machines are nearly 50% faster than the Mac Pro machines, and their results are especially interesting when it comes to integration tests. We all know that slow CI pipelines suck. However, not only will M1 machines reduce the build times of your integration tests, but they are also the way to go if you want to publish your apps on the App Store or Play Store.

        Get started with Codemagic and M1 VMs!

Items.Title: 常见的风险分析和管理策略以及为什么应该这样做 | Common Risk Analysis and Management Strategies and Why It Should be Done
Items.Description:
根据当代定义，风险是不幸事件的发生。 一旦发生，损失的可能性和可能性就会增加。 在每个软件开发生命周期中，及时交付质量不打折扣的产品非常重要。 因此，识别和预防风险是一个重要阶段。 在测试阶段，当风险管理得到有效规划时，它可以帮助所有其他任务的顺利进行。 在下面的文章中，我们将讨论风险管理的步骤以及一些常见的风险以及正确分析和预防这些风险的有效策略。

如何进行风险管理

以下是成功进行风险管理的核心步骤。

  • 应建立上下文，并应提及一些标准，根据这些标准定义风险分析结构。

  • 确定可能导致并发症的可能情况。 这些情景应构成计划进一步分析情景的基础。

  • 根据风险发生的可能性和影响范围分析风险。

  • 将估计的风险标准与已识别的风险进行比较，对它们进行排序并确定管理的优先级。

  • 首先监控和审查低优先级风险。 对于高优先级风险，制定适当的管理计划，以确保在可接受的水平上管理风险所需的所有步骤。

  • 应定期监控和审查您的风险管理系统的性能。

  • 在风险管理程序的每个步骤之后，根据需要与利益相关者协商。

让我们讨论一些常见的风险及其预防措施。

在此之前查看CSS Media Queries这是一项新的 CSS 特定技术，用于根据媒体信息应用样式。 它们可用于定位大于或小于特定尺寸的设备、具有足够带宽来处理所需媒体的设备等等。

与预计交货时间相关的风险

在敏捷方法和瀑布方法中，整个项目或单个冲刺都有特定的截止日期。 当团队或组织不遵守预定的交付时间并且未能完成冲刺或项目时，会导致组织的经济损失。 最坏的情况是客户拒绝接受项目并将任务分配给另一个供应商，从而导致公司声誉恶化。

管理和预防——通过检查以下因素可以避免预定交货风险

  • 确保计算出完成计划任务的适当时间，并向客户提供正确的估计。 还应考虑备份时间，请记住，由于资源不可用、测试用例场景不幸失败等原因，时间可能会增加。

  • 应确保正确跟踪资源。 系统、工作人员、他们的个人技能都应该被跟踪，以确保在预定时间内完成特定任务。

  • 应在需求分析期间识别复杂的功能，并计算适当的时间来开发这些功能。 如果项目是敏捷项目，则应留出额外的时间来应对项目范围和功能的意外变化。

预算估计风险

项目的预算通常会随着时间而变化。 在客户有正当理由增加预算之前，它们往往会减少。 错误地估计项目预算可能导致成本超支和项目范围扩大，间接影响交付时间。 让我们讨论如何将这种风险降到最低。

管理和预防——可以通过以下程序预防与预算相关的风险。

  • 防止镀金- 镀金是项目团队在应用程序中添加更多内容而利益相关者没有要求的情况。 尽管他们的目的是通过添加额外的功能来改进产品，但这会消耗时间并增加预算。 所以，如果客户不要求额外的东西，或者不批准预算要求，应该避免镀金。

  • 货币波动——当您与国际客户合作并且项目持续时间很长时，预算可能是固定的，但随着时间的推移，货币汇率可能会波动，您的实际预算可能会低于原来的汇率固定的。 这种情况可以通过在决定项目预算时增加一个百分比的意外事件来避免。

嘿，您在寻找CSS 运动路径吗？ 这是一个定义偏移路径属性的模块。 它使用 SVG 路径或基本形状提供动画运动路径。

技术风险应急计划

可能导致项目失败的最重要的风险类型之一是技术风险。 它们可能由于多种原因而出现，例如不断变化的需求、缺乏先进的技术或集成过程中的复杂性。 让我们讨论如何避免这种情况。

预防——

如果要使用的技术是先进的或处于初始阶段，开发团队可能还没有意识到这一点。 在实际项目工作开始之前，应计划一次适当的知识共享会议。

应遵循适当的方法来避免持续的需求更改。 在瀑布模型的情况下，客户应该了解需求规范，并且只有在给出绿色信号后才能开始工作。 在敏捷中，支持需求更改，但不应过于频繁。 在每个 sprint 结束后，应向客户展示一个演示，以确保他对开发工作的进行方式感到满意。

项目集成过程中的复杂性可以通过维护模块的适当结构、遵循命名约定以使其更易于理解并最终遵循行业制定的开发标准来避免。

另请查看css not sel list — CSS 级别 2 和 3 仅允许 :not() 伪类接受单个简单选择器。 例如，:not(a)、:not(.b) 或 :not([c]) 不起作用。 第 4 级在 :not() 中引入了对选择器列表的支持。 例如，:not(a, .b), [c]) 可以写成 :not(a), .b) 和 [c])。

风险分析的重要性

该项目的一个重要本质是风险分析和管理。 它可以帮助项目团队。

  • 避免任何重大灾难

  • 节省不必要的开支并增加收入

  • 有助于探索新领域

  • 确保交付优质产品并提高组织的声誉

如果您还没有在项目中引入风险管理，那么现在正是这样做的最佳时机，以避免任何可能导致项目失败的重大灾难。



原文:

As per contemporary definition, risk is the occurrence of unfortunate events. Once it happens, the probability and potential of loss increases. In every software development life cycle, timely delivery of the product that comes with uncompromised quality is important. Hence, identification and prevention of risk is an important phase. In the testing phase, when risk management is planned efficiently it can help in the smooth flow of all other assignments. In the following article, we shall discuss the steps of risk management and some common risks and effective strategies for properly analyzing and preventing those risks.

How Risk Management is Performed

Following are the core steps of successfully carrying out risk management.

  • A context should be established, and some criteria should be mentioned based on which the risk analyzation structure will be defined.

  • Identify the probable scenarios that may cause complications. These scenarios should form the base on which further analyzation scenarios are to be planned.

  • Analyze the risks based on their likeliness to occur and their range of impact.

  • Compare the estimated risk criterion against the identified risk, rank them and identify the priority of management.

  • Monitor and review the low priority risks first. For high priority risks, construct a proper management plan that ensures all the steps required to manage the risks at a level that is acceptable.

  • Performance of your risk managing system should be monitored and reviewed on a regular basis.

  • Consult with the stakeholders as required after each step of risk management procedure.

Let’s discuss some common risks and their prevention.

Before that check out CSS Media Queries It is a new CSS-specific technology created to apply styles based on media information. They can be used to target devices that are larger or smaller than a specific size, devices which have enough bandwidth to handle the required media and more.

Risk Associated with Estimated Delivery Time

In both Agile as well as Waterfall methodology, either the entire project or individual sprints have a specific deadline. When the team or the organization does not abide by that scheduled delivery time and fails to complete the sprint or pr oject it leads to economical loss of the organization. The worst-case scenario being when the client denies accepting the project and the assignment is given to another vendor, leading to deterioration of the company’s reputation.

Management and Prevention— Scheduled delivery risk may be avoided by checking the following factors

  • Ensure that proper time is calculated to complete the scheduled task and the correct estimation is provided to the client. Backup time should also be considered keeping in mind that time may increase due to unavailability of resources, an unfortunate failure of test case scenarios, etc.

  • Proper tracking of resources should be ensured. Systems, people working, their individual skills everything should be tracked to ensure completion of a specific task within scheduled time.

  • Complex functionalities should be identified during the requirement analysis and proper time should be calculated to develop those functionalities. If the project is in Agile, extra time should be kept at hand for unexpected change in project scope and functionalities.

Budget Estimation Risk

The budget of a project usually changes with time. And they tend to get reduced until the customer is given a valid reason to increase the budget. Wrongly estimating the budget of a project may lead to cost overrun and the expansion of project scope, indirectly impacting delivery time. Let’s discuss how this risk can be minimized.

Management and Prevention— Budget related risk can be prevented by the following procedures.

  • Preventing Gold Plating— Gold plating is the scenario when the project team adds more stuff in the application without the stakeholders asking for it. Although their intention is to improve the product by adding extra functionality, it consumes time as well as increases the budget. So, if the customer does not ask for the extra stuff, or does not approve budget requirements, gold plating should be avoided.

  • Fluctuation of currency— When you are working with an international customer and the project duration is quite long, the budget may be fixed but with time, the currency rate may get fluctuated and your actual budget may get lower compared to the rate at which it was fixed. This situation can be avoided by adding a percentage contingency while deciding the budget of the project.

Hey, are you looking for CSS Motion Path? This is a module defining the offset-path property. It provides animatable motion paths using SVG Paths or basic shapes.

Contingency Plans for Technical Risk

One of the most important risk types that may lead to project failure are technical risks. They may arise due to several reasons, like continuous requirement changes, lack of advanced technology or complexity during integration. Let’s discuss how this can be avoided.

Prevention –

If the technology to be used is advanced or in its initial stage, the development team may not be aware of it yet. A proper knowledge sharing session should be planned before the actual project work begins.

Continuous requirement changes should be avoided by following a proper methodology. In the case of the waterfall model, the customer should be aware of the requirement specification and the work should start only once the green signal is given. In Agile, requirement changes are supported, but they should not be too frequent. After the end of each sprint, a demo should be presented to the customer to make sure that he is happy with how the development work is proceeding.

Complexity during project integration can be avoided by maintaining a proper structure of the modules, following naming conventions so that they can be easier to understand and finally following the standards set by the industry for development.

Also check out css not sel list — CSS Levels 2 and 3 only allowed the :not() pseudo-class to accept a single simple selector. For instance, :not(a), :not(.b), or :not([c]) did not work. Level 4 introduces support for lists of selectors in :not(). For example, :not(a, .b), [c]) can be written as :not(a), .b) and [c]).

Importance of Risk Analysis

An important essence of the project is Risk analysis and management. It helps the project team to.

  • Avoid any major disaster

  • Save unnecessary expenses and enhance revenues

  • Helps to explore new domains

  • Ensures delivery of a quality product and increases the reputation of the organization

If you have not introduced risk management in your project, it is the perfect time to do so, in order to avoid any major disaster that may lead to project failure.

Items.Title: C++23：（有符号的）size_t 的文字后缀 | C++23: Literal suffix for (signed) size_t
Items.Description:
让我们继续探索 C++23 的特性！ 本周我们讨论对文字后缀的扩展语言支持。

什么是文字后缀？

文字可以有一个可选的后缀来指示文字的类型。 因此，不必将值存储在所需类型的变量中，而是可以直接使用文字。

例如，如果您需要一个long值并且不想依赖隐式转换，则可以传递42L而不是传递42 。

虽然我们可以定义自己的用户定义文字，但对于整数，C++ 提供了很多文字后缀：

  • 但是为什么我们需要这个新的Z字面后缀呢？

    如果你是一个几乎总是 Auto的人，当你想编写一个好的旧for循环时，你可能经常摇头。 但是，即使您只是查看了遗留代码的for循环，您也可能会看到太多与循环索引有关的混乱情况。

    我们来看一个简单的情况：

     #include <vector>
int main () { std :: vector < int > v { 0 , 1 , 2 , 3 }; for ( auto i = 0 ; i < v . size (); ++ i ) { /* use both i and v[i] */ } }

    我们尝试使用auto作为循环索引，但我们收到了编译器警告！ 同时推导出0为有符号整数。 将有符号类型与无符号类型进行比较会导致编译器警告。 希望您不会容忍项目中出现编译器警告，因此我们认为上述示例无法编译。

    万一您出于优化原因想要存储向量的大小，甚至会遇到硬错误，提醒您i和s的auto教育不一致！

     #include <vector>
int main () { std :: vector < int > v { 0 , 1 , 2 , 3 }; for ( auto i = 0 , s = v . size (); i < s ; ++ i ) { /* use both i and v[i] */ } }

    如果0u用于初始化i怎么办？ 这取决于您是否有一个辅助变量来存储大小和您的系统。

    最坏的情况是i将在 64 位系统上被截断，因为0u被推断为unsinged int ，而s是long unsigned int 。 在更好的情况下，由于以下原因，您会收到编译错误：

     #include <vector>
int main () { std :: vector < int > v { 0 , 1 , 2 , 3 }; for ( auto i = 0u , s = v . size (); i < s ; ++ i ) { /* use both i and v[i] */ } } /* main.cpp: In function 'int main()': main.cpp:5:10: error: inconsistent deduction for 'auto': 'unsigned int' and then 'long unsigned int' 5 | for (auto i = 0u, s = v.size(); i < s; ++i) { | */

    这些是从已接受提案中借用的简单示例，但您可以在其中找到更多示例。 通常，使用现有的一组文字后缀，您可能会遇到希望编译器为您推断整数文字类型的情况，因为

    • 比较有符号和无符号元素是不安全的
    • 并且您不能用ul ( unsigned long ) 替换std::size_t因为在 32 位和 64 位系统之间切换时可能会遇到缩小/截断的情况

    为了避免这些问题，您要么必须使用一些详细的强制转换（主要是static_cast ），要么引入一个辅助变量而不依赖于auto类型推导。

    如开头所述，[P0330R8] 最终通过引入Z和UZ解决了这个问题。 这样，我们之前的示例应该可以毫无问题地编译，也不会出现令人不快的意外：

     #include <vector>
int main () { std :: vector < int > v { 0 , 1 , 2 , 3 }; for ( auto i = 0U Z , s = v . size (); i < s ; ++ i ) { /* use both i and v[i] */ } }

    只需确保使用选项-std=c++2b进行编译。

    结论

    在本文中，我们看到了为什么很难对好的旧循环使用文字后缀和auto类型推导，以及P0330R8如何通过在 C++23 中引入Z / UZ来表示std::size_t来解决这种情况。

    您认为size_t的签名版本在哪里派上用场？

原文:

Let's continue our exploration of C++23 features! This week we discuss the extended language support for literal suffixes.

What is a literal suffix?

Literals can have an optional suffix which indicates the type of the literal. As such, one doesn't have to store the value in a variable of the desired type but can use the literal directly.

For example, if you need a long value and you don't want to rely on implicit conversions, you can pass 42L instead of passing 42.

While we can define our own user-defined literals, for integers, C++ provides quite a few literal suffixes:

  • none means that the literal is an int
  • U makes an integer unsigned
  • L makes integers long
  • LL males them long long
  • ULL (or LLU) turns ints into unsigned long long ints

And C++23 is going to add one, or if combined with U then 2 elements to this list:

  • Z turns an int into the sized version of std::size_t
  • UZ turns an int into std::size_t

But why do we need this new Z literal suffix?

If you're an Almost Always Auto person, you probably shook your head quite often when you wanted to write a good old for loop. But even if you just had a look into legacy code's for loops, you probably saw too many messed up situations with loop indexes.

Let's have a look at a simple situation:

#include <vector>

int main() { std::vector<int> v{0, 1, 2, 3}; for (auto i = 0; i < v.size(); ++i) { /* use both i and v[i] */ }
}

We try to use auto for the loop index, but we got a compiler warning! std::vector<T>::size() returns a std::vector<T>::size_type, usually std::size_t that is an unsigned type. At the same time, 0 is deduced as a signed integer. Comparing a signed with an unsigned type leads to a compiler warning. Hopefully, you don't tolerate compiler warnings in your project, so we consider that the above example does not compile.

In case, you want to store the size of the vector for optimization reasons, you even get a hard error, reminding you that the auto education for i and s was not consistent!

#include <vector>

int main() { std::vector<int> v{0, 1, 2, 3}; for (auto i = 0, s = v.size(); i < s; ++i) { /* use both i and v[i] */ }
}

What if 0u is used for initializing i? It depends on whether you have a helper variable to store the size and on your system.

The worst case is that i will be truncated on a 64-bit system as 0u is deduced as an unsinged int, while s is a long unsigned int. In a better situation, you get a compilation error because of this:

#include <vector>

int main() { std::vector<int> v{0, 1, 2, 3}; for (auto i = 0u, s = v.size(); i < s; ++i) { /* use both i and v[i] */ }
}
/*
main.cpp: In function 'int main()':
main.cpp:5:10: error: inconsistent deduction for 'auto': 'unsigned int' and then 'long unsigned int' 5 | for (auto i = 0u, s = v.size(); i < s; ++i) { | */

These were the simple examples borrowed from the accepted proposal, but you can find there many more. In general, with an existing set of literal suffixes, you can run into situations when you want the compiler to deduce the type for you for an integer literal because

  • comparing signed with unsigned elements is unsafe
  • and you cannot replace std::size_t with ul (unsigned long) because you can run into narrowing/truncating situations when switching between 32-bit and 64-bit systems

To avoid the problems, you either have to use some verbose casts (mostly static_cast) or introduce a helper variable without relying on auto type deduction.

As mentioned in the beginning, [P0330R8] finally solves this problem by introducing Z and UZ. Z introduces the signed version of std::size_t and UZ the unsigned version.

With that, our previous examples should compile without any problem and unpleasant surprises as such:

#include <vector>

int main() { std::vector<int> v{0, 1, 2, 3}; for (auto i = 0UZ, s = v.size(); i < s; ++i) { /* use both i and v[i] */ }
}

Just make sure that you compile with the option -std=c++2b.

Conclusion

In this article, we saw why it's difficult to use literal suffixes and auto type deduction for good old loops and how P0330R8 solves this situation by introducing Z/UZ in C++23 to denote std::size_t.

Where do you think the signed version of size_t comes in handy?

Items.Title: 2022 年适用于您的初创公司或在线业务的 5 种强大的远程工作工具 | The 5 Powerful Remote Work Tools for Your Startup or Online Business in 2022
Items.Description:
安吉特卡罗迪

发表于还记得你必须超早起床准备工作吗？ 你必须每天旅行几个小时。 这似乎是很久以前的事了，不是吗！ 您不得不乘坐长途出租车往返办公室的日子已经一去不复返了。 远程工作已成为常态。 整个办公室都驻留在您小巧紧凑的笔记本电脑中。 由于大流行，远程工作的转变相当迅速。 最初，初创公司正在努力优化他们的工作流程，因为它们通常会随着快速改进和发展而蓬勃发展。 与企业不同，初创公司要求每个人都是通才和动手。 但这变得相当困难，因为没有人接触。 那么初创公司是如何适应这种变化的呢？ 答案很简单。 初创公司利用了市场上可用的工具。 他们需要一个快速的视频会议应用程序，因此他们开始使用 Zoom。 他们需要共享数据和信息，因此他们开始使用 Evernote。 这些工具有助于弥合人与人之间的差距。 如果您是一家初创公司的一员，那么您必须提高生产力以击败竞争对手。

让我们看一些可以使您的创业或在线业务更有效率的工具。

谷歌分析

这似乎是一个非常明显的工具。 但是你会惊讶于有多少初创公司和在线企业延迟了它的整合。 即使你的创业公司不是一个网站，你也可能有一个登陆页面来获取客户。 如果您想跟踪有多少用户登陆您的页面以及他们如何与您的页面互动，您需要谷歌分析。 谷歌分析是分析网站流量的重要工具。 您可以找出哪些策略可以更好地为您的网站带来流量。 集成也毫不费力。 您必须在代码中添加几个脚本，然后观察奇迹发生。 您将了解用户的基本人口统计信息以及他们如何登陆您的页面。 根据这些信息，您可以调整您的社交媒体广告。 您可以获得详细信息，例如用户在您的网站上停留的时间以及用户导航到的所有页面。

您将获得有关用户的四种类型的信息 -

  • 一般用户数据与上述示例有关 - 用户如何登陆网站。

  • 会话数据为您提供有关用户打开网站的详细信息，计算每次访问。

  • 网页浏览数据提供有关用户访问的网页的信息。

  • 事件级别数据正在改变游戏规则。 您可以跟踪按钮点击、链接点击、视频观看等。您可以更改您的网站并从谷歌分析获得即时反馈。

无花果

这是风靡全球的工具。 您可以从浏览器访问它或下载桌面版本。 虽然 Figma 主要用于设计网站屏幕的 UI/UX，但它可以做的更多。 您可以将其用作徽标、横幅、封面等的多合一工具。

远程工作的最大问题是协作效率低下。 使用 Figma，您可以查看您的团队成员对共享文件的操作。 就像谷歌文档一样，您可以查看谁在线，甚至可以查看他们点击的位置。 它可以帮助团队对自己的工作负责。 他们觉得他们正在一起工作。 如果这些都是 Figma 提供的所有功能，它仍然是一个很棒的工具。 但它并不止于此！ 您会在任何 Slack 频道中收到有关特定文件、项目和团队的通知。 您可以获得团队提到您的所有评论的提要。您还可以使用特定项目的更新自定义 Slack 频道。

设计是任何在线业务的重要方面，而 Figma 是制作最佳设计的神奇工具。

对于新企业来说，“公共建设”是必不可少的。 它允许客户提供直接反馈。 但通常，输入不会立即收到。 这是因为它在到达开发人员之前经过了许多人。
这就是DevRev发挥作用的地方。 它直接连接开发人员和客户，即所谓的 DevCRM。 大公司需要永远解决客户面临的基本问题。 在大多数情况下，问题只是积压，很少得到解决。

即使公司决定解决这个问题，也存在多重障碍。 公司必须跨 GitHub 代码库、项目跟踪器、票务系统和 DevOps 工具管理数据。

借助DevRev ，开发人员可以参与其中。 他们直接与客户联系，因此可以立即获得反馈。 假设，作为用户，您在注册时遇到问题，并且在输入您的详细信息时页面会刷新。 您在网上任何地方都找不到解决方案。 你已经尝试过各种各样的事情，比如重新启动、刷新，甚至可能是祈祷。 现在想象一下，如果您可以直接联系该功能的制造商。 这不是解决问题的最好方法吗？ 如果有人能解决问题，那就是制造者。 所有这些都使 DevRev 成为您的在线业务或创业公司不可或缺的一部分。 以产品和客户为中心，一切都像运转良好的机器一样运转。 没有烦恼，只有快乐。

让用户感到被重视是击败竞争对手的必经之路！

特雷罗

这是一个非常直观的工具，并利用了板。 看板可让您一目了然地查看所有任务。 您将了解所有剩余的工作、谁在做什么以及每项任务的完成程度。 组织和规划通常很费力。 它使销售、研究和培训等所有无聊的事情变得令人兴奋。 将 Trello 视为类固醇的概念。 最后的想法

新的创业公司是混乱的，成功的道路从来都不是直线的。 上面的工具可以帮助您的初创公司获得一些组织并远离您的竞争对手。

Jan Schenk (he/him) - Jan Schenk (他/他) - May 23 5月23日



原文:

Ankit Karody

Posted on May 25

Remember when you had to get up super early to get ready for work? You had to travel a couple of hours every day. It seems like ages ago, doesn't it! Gone are the days when you had to take long cab rides to and from the office. Remote work has become the norm. The whole office resides in your small, compact laptop.  The change to remote work was a rather quick one because of the pandemic. Initially, startups were struggling to optimize their workflow, as they usually thrive with rapid improvements and development.  Unlike corporates, startups require everyone to be a generalist and hands-on. But this became quite hard because of no human contact.  So how were startups able to adapt to this change?  The answer is quite simple. Startups made use of the tools available in the markets. They needed a fast video conferencing app, so they started using Zoom. They needed to share data and information, so they started using Evernote.  These kinds of tools helped to bridge the gap between people.  If you are part of a startup, it is essential for you to up your productivity game to beat the competition. 

Let's look at some of the tools that can make your startup or online business more productive.

Google Analytics

This seems like a pretty obvious tool. But you'd be surprised by how many startups and online businesses delay its integration.  Even if your startup is not a website, you'll probably have a landing page to acquire customers. If you want to track how many users are landing on your page and how they interact with your page, you need google analytics.  Google Analytics is an essential tool to analyze your website traffic. You can figure out what strategies work better to drive traffic to your website.  Integration is also effortless. You have to add a couple of scripts to your code and watch the magic happen.  You get to know the basic demographic of the user and how they landed on your page. Based on this information, you can tweak your social media ads.  The best part of Google Analytics is that you get information about how the user interacts with the website. You get details such as how long the user stays on your website and all the pages a user navigates to. 

You get four types of information about the user -

  • General user data pertains to the example mentioned above - how the user landed on the website.

  • Session data gives you details about how much the user is opening the website, counting each individual visit.

  • Pageview data gives information about the pages a user visits.

  • Event level data is game-changing. You can track button clicks, link clicks, video views, etc. You can change up your website and get instant feedback from google analytics.

Figma

This is the tool that has taken the world by storm. Figma is an interface design tool that you can access from any Linux, Mac, or Windows system. You can access it from the browser or download the desktop version.  While Figma is primarily used for designing the UI/UX for Website screens, it can do much more. You can use it as your all-in-one tool for logos, banners, covers, etc. 

The biggest problem with remote work is inefficient collaboration. Figma tackles this issue with such grace that you feel like your colleague is sitting right next to you.

With Figma, you can see what your teammates are doing with shared files. Just like google docs, you can see who's online and even where they click.  It helps teams feel accountable for their work. They feel like they are working in person together.  If these were all the features Figma offered, it would still be a great tool. But it doesn't stop here!  The collaboration with Slack in March 2022 is revolutionary. You get notifications about particular files, projects, and teams in any Slack channel.  You can get a feed of all the comments your team mentioned you in. You can also customize Slack channels with updates for specific projects. 

Design is a vital aspect of any online business, and Figma is the magic tool to make the best designs.

DevRev

For new businesses, "building in public" is essential. It allows customers to give direct feedback. But usually, the input is not received immediately. This is because it travels across many people before reaching the developers. 
This is where DevRev comes into play. It directly connects the developer and the customer, a so-called DevCRM.  Big companies take forever to fix essential issues faced by customers. In most cases, the problems just become backlogs and are rarely fixed. 

Even if the company decides to fix the issue, there are multiple barriers. Companies must manage data across GitHub code repos, project trackers, ticketing systems, and DevOps tools.

With DevRev, developers have their skin in the game. They directly connect with the customers and, therefore, get immediate feedback.  Suppose, as a user, you had a problem on sign up and the page refreshes when entering your details. You can't find solutions anywhere online. You've tried all kinds of things like restarting, refreshing, and maybe even praying.  Now imagine if you could directly contact the maker of the feature. Wouldn't that be the best way to solve the issue? If anyone can solve the problem, it is the maker.  All of this makes DevRev integral for your online business or startup. With the product and the customer at the center, everything runs like a well-oiled machine.   No hassle, only happiness. 

Making the user feel valued is a sure way to beat the competition!

Trello

Trello is a tool that helps teams organize workflows, create deadlines, track tasks, and much more. It is a very visual tool and makes use of boards.  Boards allow you to see all tasks at a glance. Trello organizes everything in a fun way with lists and cards. You get to know all the work left, who's working on what, and the level of completion of every task.  Organization and planning are usually taxing. Trello makes all of this simple. It makes all the boring stuff like sales, research, and training exciting. Think of Trello as Notion on steroids. Trello has multiple integrations such as Slack, MailChimp, GitHub, Google Drive, Salesforce, etc. You can use Trello in tandem with your other tools to become a productivity powerhouse. 

Trello is easy to use, and the best part is that it is affordable.

Final Thoughts

New startups are chaotic, and the road to success is never linear. The tools above can help your startup get some organization and race away from your competition.

Jan Schenk (he/him) - May 23

Items.Title: 使用 Dagger 进行条件配置 | Conditional Provisioning with Dagger
Items.Description:
在本文中，您将学习如何使用 Dagger 有条件地预置（提供）您的依赖项，而不会给您的记忆增加压力。

随着您的产品的增长。 你经常开始用它尝试新事物。 由于我们并不总是知道什么对用户最有效，因此我们经常尝试多种策略来解决问题。 这是通过某种 A/B 测试完成的。 然而，这是有代价的。 代价是在同一版本的应用程序中存在两种不同的解决方案，以解决一个客户痛点。 让我们尝试通过图像编辑应用程序的镜头来观察它。

介绍示例应用程序

想象一下，您有一个允许用户编辑和分享照片的应用程序。 为了编辑照片，我们使用了一个名为SimplePhotoEditor的库。 这个库可能是您的内部实现、第三方专有解决方案，甚至是您正在使用的开源项目，但这无关紧要，我想把它排除在外。

现在您遇到了一个名为ComplexPhotoEditor的库，与市场上的替代产品相比，它为您提供更好的照片编辑功能并承诺更高的性能。

由于您是一位非常敬业且充满热情的工程师，因此您决定要在您的应用程序中试用这个新库，看看您的用户是否发现您的应用程序更有用、更健壮等等。

了解示例用例

这两个库在初始化后都需要大量内存，并且希望在应用程序会话期间的整个生命周期中保留大量内存。 不打算评论图书馆的设计：]

因此，一旦有人初始化两个库中的任何一个的对象，我们就会看到资源分配的显着峰值。 由于我们在任何时候都至少需要这两个库中的一个，因此我们将不得不承受这种开销，但是，让它们都占用它们公平份额的资源并没有真正的意义。 因此，在下一节中，我们将研究如何实现此行为。

实施用例

假设我们有一个名为 ImageManipulationStrategy 的接口，它有一个操作输入和返回输出的方法。

 interface ImageManipulationStrategy { fun manipulate(image: Image): Image }

为了支持这两个库，我们在我们的应用程序中有各自的实现，看起来像这样

class SimpleImageManipulationStrategy( private val SimpleImageEditor: simpleImageEditor ): ImageManipulationStrategy { override fun manipulate(image: Image): Image { // manipulate with simpleImageEditor } }

还有这个

class ComplexImageManipulationStrategy( private val ComplexImageEditor: complexImageEditor ): ImageManipulationStrategy { override fun manipulate(image: Image): Image { // manipulate with complexImageEditor } }

现在，当在其他类中使用这些策略时，我们可以像这样在 Dagger 模块中轻松交换它们

@Provides fun imageManipulationStrategy( simpleImageManipulationStrategy:SimpleImageManipulationStrategy, complexImageManipulationStrategy:ComplexImageManipulationStrategy, ) : ImageManipulationStrategy { // provide the desired strategy }

虽然这工作正常，但以这种方式提供依赖项存在问题，我们将在下一节中看到。

理解问题

正如我们所见，运行时的 Dagger 不会知道我们实际上可能在我们的方法中获得哪个依赖项，因此它会初始化它们。 因此，无论我们需要哪个，都可以在该方法中随时使用。

当我们只知道需要一个时，将它们都初始化，这就是问题所在。 现在，这可能发生在任何情况下，初始化一个类的成本很高，您可能必须在这些实现之间进行选择，至少在其中一个上浪费资源。

这个问题的解决方案非常简单，我们将在下一节中看到。

了解解决方案

因此，我们可以通过使用这个类来推迟初始化，而不是使用提供依赖项的默认机制。

它的工作方式是 Dagger 将为您Provider<T>对象，您可以使用它来get()实际依赖项。

这是它在实践中的样子

@Provides fun imageManipulationStrategy( simpleProvider: Provider<SimpleImageManipulationStrategy>, complexProvider: Provider<ComplexImageManipulationStrategy>, ) : ImageManipulationStrategy { return if (myConditionIsMet) { simpleProvider.get() } else { complexProvider.get() } }

这将确保 Dagger 在我们不需要时不会不必要地初始化依赖项。

结论

  • 您并不总是希望依赖 Dagger 直接为您提供依赖项。
  • 您应该始终评估您的任何依赖项是否需要资源。
  • 可以使用 Dagger 在运行时有条件地在图表中提供依赖项。
  • 当使用Provider<T>的对象时，除非调用该对象的get()方法，否则 Dagger 不会初始化依赖项。

最初于 2022 年 4 月 2 日发布于https://www.droidchef.dev 。



原文:

In this article, you’ll learn how to use Dagger for conditionally provisioning (providing) your dependencies without adding stress to your memory.

As your product grows. You often start trying out new things with it. Since we don’t always know what’ll work best for the users, we often try multiple strategies to solve a problem. This is done through some sort of an A/B test. However, this comes at a cost. The cost is that two distinct solutions exist in the same version of the app, to solve one customer pain point. Let’s try to see it through a lens of an image editing application.

Introducing the Sample App

Imagine you have an app that allows users to edit photos and share them. For editing photos, we use a library called SimplePhotoEditor. This library could be your in-house implementation, a third-party proprietary solution, or even an open-source project that you are using, but that is irrelevant and I wanted to get that out of the way.

Now you come across a library called ComplexPhotoEditor which offers you better photo editing capabilities and promises higher performance when compared to its alternatives in the market.

As you are a very dedicated and passionate engineer, you decide that you want to give this new library a try in your app and see if your users find your app to be more useful, robust, and whatnot.

Understanding the Sample Use Case

Both the libraries require a lot of memory as soon as they are initialized and want to hold on to a significant amount of it, throughout their lifetime during the app’s session. Not going to comment on the design of the library :]

So as soon as someone initializes an object of either of the two libraries we’d see a significant spike in resource allocation. Since we need at least one of the two libraries at all given times, we’ll have to live with that overhead, however, having both of them taking up their fair share of resources doesn’t really make sense. So in the next section, we’ll look into implementing this behavior.

Implementing the Use Case

Let’s say we have an interface called ImageManipulationStrategy which has a method to manipulate the input and return an output.

interface ImageManipulationStrategy { 
 fun manipulate(image: Image): Image 
}

In order to support both the libraries, we have their respective implementations in our app, that look like this

class SimpleImageManipulationStrategy(
 private val SimpleImageEditor: simpleImageEditor
    ): ImageManipulationStrategy {

     override fun manipulate(image: Image): Image {
         // manipulate with simpleImageEditor
     }
}

and this

class ComplexImageManipulationStrategy(
 private val ComplexImageEditor: complexImageEditor
    ): ImageManipulationStrategy {

     override fun manipulate(image: Image): Image {
         // manipulate with complexImageEditor
     }
}

Now when using these strategies in other classes we can easily swap them in our Dagger Module like this

@Provides
fun imageManipulationStrategy(
  simpleImageManipulationStrategy:SimpleImageManipulationStrategy,
  complexImageManipulationStrategy:ComplexImageManipulationStrategy,
) : ImageManipulationStrategy {
    // provide the desired strategy
}

While this works fine, there is a problem with providing the dependency this way and we’ll see that in the next section.

Understanding the Problem

As we can see Dagger at Runtime will not know which dependency we actually might get in our method, so it’ll initialize both of them. So that whichever one we need, is readily available at our disposal in the method.

Having both of them initialized when we only know one is going to be needed, is the problem here. Now, this can occur in any case, where initializing a class is expensive and you might have to choose between those implementations, wasting resources on at least one of them.

The solution to this problem is pretty simple and we’ll see that in the next section.

Understanding the Solution

Dagger exposes the Provider<T> class to us. So instead of using the default mechanism of providing the dependencies, we can defer the initialization by using this class.

The way it works is that Dagger will provide you the Provider<T> object and you can use it to get() the actual dependency.

Here is how it’d look in practice

@Provides
fun imageManipulationStrategy(
    simpleProvider: Provider<SimpleImageManipulationStrategy>,
    complexProvider: Provider<ComplexImageManipulationStrategy>,
) : ImageManipulationStrategy {
    return if (myConditionIsMet) {
        simpleProvider.get()
    } else {
        complexProvider.get()
    }
}

and this will ensure that Dagger doesn’t initialize a dependency unnecessarily when we don’t need it to.

Conclusion

  • You don’t always want to rely on Dagger to provision dependencies for you directly.
  • You should always assess if any of your dependencies are hungry for resources.
  • It is possible to conditionally provision dependencies in your graph at runtime, with Dagger.
  • Dagger exposes a class called Provider
  • When using an object of Provider<T>, Dagger won't initialize the dependency unless the get() method on this object is called.

Originally published at https://www.droidchef.dev on April 2, 2022.
Items.Author: droidchef

Items.Title: 在 Python 中自动化记事本和 Excel 应用程序 | Automating Notepad and Excel applications in Python
Items.Description:
一遍又一遍地执行重复性任务可能会非常无聊和乏味。 大多数人喜欢接受挑战，然后因复杂的任务而获得奖励，这使他们的技能组合有所增长。 这就是自动化的用武之地。自动化重复性任务可以为其他重要事情节省宝贵的时间。 只要具备一点 Python 编程知识，就可以让计算机自动高效地为您完成这些任务。

在 windows 平台中，可以使用 Python 脚本自动执行不同的任务，这些任务包括启动记事本、在 Excel 电子表格中读取和写入数据。 要使用 Python 实现自动化，您需要了解什么是自动化，以及什么是 Python。 自动化描述了通过确定决策标准、子流程、相关操作以及它们之间的关联方式来减少流程中人为干预的各种技术。 另一方面，Python 是一种高级通用编程语言，具有友好、平易近人的语法和可用于任务自动化的库基调。

在本文中，我们将学习如何使用 Pywinauto 模块在 Windows 中自动化无聊的东西。 它允许您将输入操作发送到 Windows 对话框和控件。

目录

先决条件

要继续阅读，读者必须对Python有很好的了解。

可以使用自动化的领域

在继续之前，让我们看一下一些最常见的自动化场景：

  • 来自自动化支持中心的服务： - 各种公司或组织提供帮助中心，客户可以通过电子邮件、短信或其他方式提出问题并获得快速响应。
  • 自动填写表格： - 自动化脚本可以轻松处理填写在线表格和恢复忘记的凭据等繁琐的工作。
  • 快速气候报告： - 您也可以简单快速地查看天气，但如果它是自动发送给您的，效率会更高。 因此，这是一个自动化案例，您会自动收到每日天气更新的通知。
  • 每日自动邮件触发： - 如果您必须每天发送附件，这很有用。 这可以通过每天运行的自动脚本轻松管理。 要验证您的安装，请运行以下代码片段：

     from pywinauto.application import Application app = Application ( backend = "uia" ). start ( "notepad.exe" ) # open notepad app app . UntitledNotepad . type_keys ( "%FX" )

    这应该会在 Windows 界面上启动您的记事本应用程序。
    在此安装和验证之后，我们将跳转到记事本自动化，这是一种简单的技术，然后是 excel 自动化，我们将从电子表格中读取数据并将数据写入 Excel 单元格。

    自动化记事本

    这个示例将通过查看启动应用程序、最大化和最小化应用程序以及在记事本应用程序中写入文本等功能，让我们深入了解 pywinauto 的功能。 首先，让我们首先导入相关字段并使用导入的应用程序的启动方法启动记事本。

     from pywinauto import application ap = application . Application () ap . start ( 'Notepad.exe' )

    当执行上述代码时，记事本应用程序就会启动。 让我们在打开的记事本应用程序上添加一些文本。 我们打算显示的消息在type_key()s方法内的文本区域中作为字符串传递。

     text_message = "Hello, this is a sample text for python automation" dlg . typekeys ( text_message , with_spaces = True )

    我们可以使用is_maximize ()方法来最大化记事本窗口，因为在大多数情况下，当记事本启动时，它的窗口并不总是全屏。

     #maximize the notepad window if dlg . is_maximized () == False : dlg . maximize ()

    输出 ：

    使用 Pywinauto 自动化 Excel

    在这里，我们将学习如何在 Excel 文件上自动映射和写入数据。 首先，我们从访问 excel 电子表格开始。

     program_path = r "C:Program Files (x86)Microsoft OfficerootOffice16EXCEL.exe" #exel application path file_path = r "C:Users\Desktop\mysample.xlsx" # .xlsx file location

    我们现在通过 excel.exe 打开我们的.xlsx文件。

     app = application . Application ( backend = "uia" ). start ( r '{} "{}"' . format ( program_path , file_path ))

    正如我们对记事本所做的那样，我们现在必须选择我们需要处理的窗口 mysample.xlxs。 然后打印结果。

    我们可以select ()索引窗口，也可以通过传递名称直接获取它。

     dlg = app [ 'mysample.xlsx-Excel' ]

    我们现在将自动从 Excel 表中读取数据并用一句话打印出来。

    我们在单元格A1 、 B1和C1中有数据条目，因此单元格A1的自动化 ID 是A1 ， D1的自动化 ID 是D1 。 用于读取数据并将结果写入语句的代码是：

     tXt = "" for j in range 65 , 65 + 5 ): ex_id = str ( chr ( j )) + str ( 1 ) dlg . child_window ( auto_id = ex_id ). click_input () ein = dlg . child_window ( auto_id = ex_id ) ein . click_input () txt += ( ein . legacy_properties ()[ 'Value' ]) txt += " " print ( "Text read from mysample.xlsx is:" ,( txt ))

    结果 ：

     The text read from mysample.xlsx is: student name admission number year of study

    我们为每个单元格创建的自动化 ID 在 for 循环dlg. child_window () 然后我们使用click input ()进行鼠标单击以抓取该元素。 我们现在将在单元格A4中写入数据：

     write_data = "John Doe" window = dlg . child_window ( auto_id = "A4" ) window . click_input () window . type_keys ( write_data , with_spaces = True )

    输出：

    这里我们需要写入的字符串是write_data 。 然后我们抓取元素并使用点击输入点击它。 要在单元格A4中写入数据，我们使用type_keys方法，该方法接受两个参数，即要写入的字符串，并检查输入数据中的空格。

    下面我们将介绍一些用于自动化计算机化工作的重要工具。

    它利用简单邮件传输协议 (SMTP)，该协议可轻松与大多数主要电子邮件提供商（如 Gmail）兼容。

    硒

    这个包允许您从 Python 控制网站浏览器活动。 您可以从此处的官方文档中阅读有关 Selenium 的更多信息

    美丽汤

    该模块用于获取 HTML 和 XML 文件中的数据。 您可以从此处的官方文档中阅读有关 Beautiful Soup 的更多信息

    结论

    在本教程中，我们了解了 Pywinauto 是什么，以及如何自动化记事本和 Excel。 我们还研究了一些 Python 自动化工具，例如 selenium、smtlib 等。您可以从这里进一步了解不同的概念。

    延伸阅读

    有关 Pywinauto 的更多信息，请访问以下页面：

    • 自动化应用程序
